changes: ZeroClaw added another same-cycle release with v0.6.2, extending the burst that already produced v0.6.0 and v0.6.1 yesterday.
nanobot elevated a litellm supply-chain poisoning advisory to the top of its README and pointed users to the dependency-removal commit 3dfdab7.
PicoClaw pushed a fresher nightly build, while OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, NanoClaw, IronClaw, and AstrBot all showed continued repo movement without a taxonomy-changing announcement.
The monitored set still did not need to change: Hermes remains the canonical tracked repo at https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the current positioning map still holds.
whyItMatters: The clearest new signal today is security posture becoming part of the competitive story, not just feature velocity.
ZeroClaw’s v0.6.2 keeps reinforcing the same wedge: fast release tempo plus a low-footprint Rust runtime remains one of the strongest derivative narratives in the market.
The broader field is still active, but today looks more like ecosystem continuation and hardening than a new category turn.
This is meaningful enough for the daily record, but not enough to redraw the stable strategic read.
